assumptions: Caller supplies dest EID/TO/tag/msg-type stable per message; source EID programmed via CSR before intake
Egress is a generic packet/AXI-Stream sink that may apply backpressure
confirmed_scope: REQ_TX_INTAKE: streaming message intake with last-flag and sideband attribute word (dest EID/tag/TO/msg-type); opaque body
REQ_TX_FRAGMENT: fragment body into MTU-bounded packets, short final packet, single-packet case
REQ_TX_SOM_EOM: SOM on first, EOM on last, single-packet SOM=EOM=1
REQ_TX_SEQNUM: 2-bit packet sequence number 0 then +1 mod 4
REQ_TX_HEADER: header version fixed, caller dest EID/TO/tag, CSR source EID, IP SOM/EOM/seq, DSP0236 byte layout, order-preserving body
REQ_TX_SINGLEMSG: one message at a time, no interleaving
REQ_TX_ERR: egress backpressure stall, underrun/oversize/zero-length flag+count, no partial emission
REQ_TX_STATUS: done IRQ, error IRQ, sent/dropped counters over APB-like CSR
REQ_TX_RESET: async active-low assert / sync deassert, idle/seq0/counters-clear/CSR defaults
open_questions: CSR address map and IRQ enable semantics (DT008 sub-detail)
Oversize threshold / max message length (DT007 sub-detail)
summary: MCTP TX Assembler v0: message intake (streaming+last-flag+attr word), MTU fragmentation, SOM/EOM, seq# mod4, header composition/ownership/byte-order, single-message-at-a-time, error/backpressure handling, status/IRQ/CSR, reset/default state.
